messages.content:
in 35 word answer this question, What are the differences between the ‘ad hoc’ and ‘BSS’ modes of WLAN? using this reference: Sauter, M. (2021). From GSM to LTE-advanced pro and 5G an introduction to mobile networks and
Mobile Broadband. Wiley.

Ad hoc mode is a spontaneous connection between wireless devices without a central access point, whereas BSS mode involves a central access point serving as the communication hub for the wireless devices. (35 words)

in 40 word answer this question, What are the differences between the ‘ad hoc’ and ‘BSS’ modes of WLAN? use this reference: Sauter, M. (2021). From GSM to LTE-advanced pro and 5G an introduction to mobile networks and Mobile Broadband. Wiley.

Ad hoc mode is a peer-to-peer connection between wireless devices, while in BSS mode, wireless devices connect to a central access point. Ad hoc mode supports direct communication between devices, whereas BSS mode enables communication with devices outside the immediate range. (44 words)

in 40 words answer this question, Which additional functionalities can often be found in WLAN APs? use this reference: Sauter, M. (2021). From GSM to LTE-advanced pro and 5G an introduction to mobile networks and Mobile Broadband. Wiley.

WLAN access points (APs) often contain additional functionalities such as network security, Quality of Service (QoS), and support for multiple SSIDs, VLANs, and user authentication methods to ensure efficient and secure wireless network operation. (36 words)
in 35 words answer this question, What is an ESS? use this reference: Sauter, M. (2021). From GSM to LTE-advanced pro and 5G an introduction to mobile networks and Mobile Broadband. Wiley.
Extended Service Set (ESS) is a network of BSSs interconnected by distribution systems that creates a larger coverage area and enables mobility for wireless devices to roam seamlessly between BSSs.

in 35 words answer this question, What is an SSID and in which frames is it used? use this reference: Sauter, M. (2021). From GSM to LTE-advanced pro and 5G an introduction to mobile networks and Mobile Broadband. Wiley.

Service Set Identifier (SSID) is a unique identifier for a WLAN service set that enables wireless devices to identify and connect to a specific WLAN. It is used in Beacon frames and Probe Response frames.

in 40 words answer this question, What kinds of PS mechanisms exist in the WLAN standard? use this reference: Sauter, M. (2021). From GSM to LTE-advanced pro and 5G an introduction to mobile networks and Mobile Broadband. Wiley.

Power Saving (PS) mechanisms in WLANs include Inactivity PS, PS-Poll mode, and scheduled PS. Inactivity PS turns off a device's radio after a period of inactivity, PS-Poll mode sends PS-Poll frames to request buffered frames, and scheduled PS schedules a predetermined wake-up time for the device.

in 40 words answer this question, Why are ACK frames used in a WLAN? use this reference: Sauter, M. (2021). From GSM to LTE-advanced pro and 5G an introduction to mobile networks and Mobile Broadband. Wiley.

ACK frames are used in WLANs to provide reliable communications. After receiving a data frame, an ACK frame is transmitted back to the sender to confirm the successful transmission. If no ACK frame is received, the sender will retransmit the frame.

in 40 words answer this question, Why do 802.11g networks use the RTS/CTS mechanism? use this reference: Sauter, M. (2021). From GSM to LTE-advanced pro and 5G an introduction to mobile networks and Mobile Broadband. Wiley.

802.11g networks use the RTS/CTS mechanism to prevent collisions and improve network efficiency. By using Request to Send (RTS) and Clear to Send (CTS) frames, stations avoid transmitting data frames simultaneously and reduce collisions caused by hidden nodes.

in 32 words answer this question, Why are three MAC addresses required in BSS frame? use this reference: Sauter, M. (2021). From GSM to LTE-advanced pro and 5G an introduction to mobile networks and Mobile Broadband. Wiley.
Three MAC addresses are required in BSS frames to enable wireless devices to identify the source, destination, and BSS ID when communicating with other devices in the WLAN.

in 32 words answer this question, How can a receiving device detect the speed at which the payload part of a frame was sent? use this reference: Sauter, M. (2021). From GSM to LTE-advanced pro and 5G an introduction to mobile networks and Mobile Broadband. Wiley.

A receiving device can detect the speed of the payload transmission by comparing the length of the frame with the time it took to receive the frame, based on the data rate and duration information in the received frame headers.

in 32 words answer this question, What is the maximum transfer rate that can be reached in a data transfer between two 802.11g devices in a BSS? use this reference: Sauter, M. (2021). From GSM to LTE-advanced pro and 5G an introduction to mobile networks and Mobile Broadband. Wiley.
The maximum transfer rate in an 802.11g data transfer between two devices in a BSS is 54 Mbps, which is achieved through the use of OFDM and the 20 MHz channel bandwidth.

in 40 words answer this question, What disadvantages does the DCF method have for telephony and video streaming applications? use this reference: Sauter, M. (2021). From GSM to LTE-advanced pro and 5G an introduction to mobile networks and Mobile Broadband. Wiley.

The Distributed Coordination Function (DCF) method can lead to delay variation, or jitter, causing poor quality for telephony and video streaming applications. It also does not provide deterministic latency and limited capacity for real-time data delivery.

in 20 words answer this question, Which security holes exist in the WEP procedures? use this reference: Sauter, M. (2021). From GSM to LTE-advanced pro and 5G an introduction to mobile networks and Mobile Broadband. Wiley.
There are several security holes in the WEP procedure, such as the use of a weak initialization vector and weak encryption algorithms.

in 45 words answer this question, Which security holes exist in the WEP procedures, and how are they solved by WPA and WPA2 (802.1x)? use this reference: Sauter, M. (2021). From GSM to LTE-advanced pro and 5G an introduction to mobile networks and Mobile Broadband. Wiley.

The WEP procedure has several security weaknesses such as weak encryption and weak initialization vectors. These weaknesses are addressed in WPA and WPA2 by using stronger encryption protocols, the four-way handshake authentication, and 802.1x authentication.
